questions.question: Which Twitter card type renders a large hero image alongside the post title and description?
questions.explanation: `summary_large_image` is the variant with a wide hero image - typically what an engineering blog with custom share images wants. `summary` shows only a small square thumbnail.

questions.question: You set `image: /assets/img/posts/foo.png` on a post and the Facebook Sharing Debugger reports no image found. Why?
choices: The path needs to be absolute (include scheme and host); relative paths are not resolved by social scrapers
Open Graph images must be exactly 1200×630 - any other size is rejected
`og:image` cannot be set from front matter; it must be hardcoded in the layout
questions.explanation: Social scrapers refuse relative paths. The URL needs scheme and host - use `absolute_url` in Liquid or let `jekyll-seo-tag` prefix it via `site.url`. Size, extension, and front-matter sourcing are all flexible.

questions.question: You updated a post's social image yesterday, but LinkedIn still shows the old one when you paste the URL. What's the best first action?
choices: Wait 30 days for the platform's cache to expire naturally
Rename the post URL so LinkedIn treats it as new content
Run the URL through LinkedIn's Post Inspector to force a re-scrape
Delete the old image file from your repo so the broken-image fallback kicks in
questions.explanation: Every social platform caches scrape results aggressively. The debugger/inspector tools let you force a re-fetch - that's their primary use. Renaming the URL works but breaks links; waiting is correct but slow; deleting the file makes things worse.
